headline: HP, Intel, and Xerox Are All Chasing the Same Comeback. History Says Only One Survives
summary: HP, Intel, and Xerox all built the hardware world we grew up in, but the market now prices them as if they belong to different centuries. History offers a precise template for which kind of struggling giant survives long enough to matter again, and only one of these three fits it.

content.title: HP, Intel, and Xerox Are All Chasing the Same Comeback. History Says Only One Survives

headline: Comcast, Alphabet, Palantir, SpaceX, Microsoft, and More Stocks That Explain Today’s Market
summary: Technology stocks were trading higher in premarket trading Monday as the  looked set to finally snap its five-day losing streak.  Semiconductor companies, other artificial-intelligence stocks, and the Magnificent Seven were among those rebounding to start the week.  Microsoft  jumped 2% ahead of the open, while  Amazon Alphabet Nvidia Meta  and  Tesla  were all up around 1%.

headline: Alphabet joins Dow Jones Industrial Average, shares rise 3.7%
summary: Investing.com -- Alphabet Inc. (NASDAQ:GOOGL) shares rose 3.7% to $350.24 on Monday as the Google parent company began trading as a member of the Dow Jones Industrial Average, replacing Verizon Communications (NYSE:VZ).

headline: Alphabet debuts in Dow Jones Industrial Average as index tilts toward tech
summary: Alphabet shares climbed on Monday as the Google parent made its debut in the blue-chip Dow Jones ‌Industrial Average, replacing Verizon Communications, and immediately ranking among its ‌most influential members.  • Its shares rose 3.7% to $350.24, offering one of the biggest boosts ​to the 30-member Dow.  • The company replaced Verizon on the index, S&P Dow Jones Indices said in an announcement on June 23.

content.title: Alphabet debuts in Dow Jones Industrial Average as index tilts toward tech

headline: ByteDance targets CPU mass production in H2 2027 to power AI push - SCMP
summary: Investing.com -- ByteDance is racing to finalize the design of a next-generation in-house CPU by early 2027 at the latest, with mass production and wider deployment targeted for the second half of that year, South China Morning Post reported Monday, citing three people familiar with the matter. The disclosure, the first of its kind for the TikTok parent, underscores how urgently one of the world's most valuable private tech companies is building out its own silicon stack to sustain explosive gro

headline: The Real Price Of Intel Stock Is Three Years Away
summary: At a glance, Intel (INTC) stock looks expensive. Trading around $128.32, the shares command a multiple of about 118x this year’s expected earnings. For many investors, that’s where the analysis stops. A triple-digit price-to-earnings ratio is often a dealbreaker, suggesting a price tag that has far outrun the business fundamentals.

content.title: The Real Price Of Intel Stock Is Three Years Away
content.summary: At a glance, Intel (INTC) stock looks expensive. Trading around $128.32, the shares command a multiple of about 118x this year’s expected earnings. headline: Wall Street Just Supersized Its Price Target on Intel. Is the Stock Still Too Cheap?
summary: Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) stock is trading at $130.60 midday Monday, up 2% on the session. The move follows a major price-target hike from Cantor Fitzgerald, which raised its target on the shares to $150 from $90 while keeping its rating at Neutral. That nuance matters. A supersized target without a rating change signals strong conviction in ... Wall Street Just Supersized Its Price Target on Intel. Is the Stock Still Too Cheap?

headline: The Margin Squeeze Threatening Intel Stock
summary: If you hold Intel (INTC) stock, you’ve had a strong year. The shares have posted a 478% gain over the last 12 months, and the narrative has shifted in the company’s favor. Management now sees “clear signs that the CPU is reinserting itself as the indispensable foundation of the AI era.” The market has bought into this comeback story, with the stock trading near 91% of its 52-week high.
